label: PASS
rationale: The target Get requests Locking Range1 columns 3 through 9 while authenticated as Admins. Opal preconfiguration for Locking_Range1 has RangeStart 0, RangeLength 0, all four boolean lock fields false, and LockOnReset Power Cycle ({0}); the cited ACE permits Admins to Get RangeStart through ActiveKey, so the returned cells match the readable stored row values.

label: FAIL
rationale: No successful Locking Set precedes the target Get, so Locking_Range1 remains at the Opal preconfigured values. Table 45 gives RangeLength as 0, not 8; the rest of the returned cells match, but column 4 is wrong.

label: PASS
rationale: The second successful Locking Set writes columns 3=120, 4=12, 5=false, 6=true, 7=true, 8=false, and 9={0,3}. A successful Set must make all submitted changes, and the Admins Get ACE covers these columns, so the target Get correctly returns those stored values. ReadLocked is operationally disregarded when ReadLockEnabled is false, but the cited text does not make that stored cell read back as false.

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior successful Locking Set changed LockOnReset to {0,3}. Because successful Set must apply all submitted changes and Get returns readable stored cells, column 9 should read back {0,3}; returning only {0} is inconsistent with the current row state.

label: PASS
rationale: After the full successful Set, a later successful partial Set changes only WriteLockEnabled to false and WriteLocked to true. The unchanged cells 5, 7, and 9 therefore remain true, true, and {0,3}, while columns 6 and 8 read back false and true. The target output matches that stored state; WriteLocked being ignored when WriteLockEnabled is false affects media write behavior, not the stored cell returned by Get.

label: FAIL
rationale: The later successful partial Set explicitly writes column 8 WriteLocked to true while writing column 6 WriteLockEnabled to false. Since successful Set applies all submitted changes and no cited rule resets the stored WriteLocked cell merely because WriteLockEnabled is false, the Get should return column 8 as true; the target returns false.

label: PASS
rationale: The successful Locking Set writes columns 3=64, 4=16, 5=true, 6=true, 7=false, 8=false, and an empty LockOnReset set. Core LockOnReset text defines an empty set as meaningful, and successful Set requires all submitted changes to be made, so the target Get correctly returns the stored empty set and other changed values.

label: FAIL
rationale: The prior successful Locking Set wrote LockOnReset as an empty set. Core defines the empty set behavior for LockOnReset, and successful Set must apply all submitted changes, so a subsequent readable Get should return column 9 as [] rather than {0}.
